title: Vitalist Bay 2026 Opens This Week — World's Largest Longevity Festival
description: Vitalist Bay 2026 begins Thursday May 14 at Berkeley's Lighthaven campus, drawing 60+ speakers across six themes including AI x Bio, Replacement, and Investment. The four-day pop-up festival is widely viewed as the field's biggest catalyst week of the year, with several preclinical and clinical readouts queued for the venue.

title: BGE-102 Phase 1 Data Reframes NLRP3 Inflammation as a Longevity Target
description: BioAge's R&D Day Phase 1 readout for BGE-102 showed profound hsCRP reductions and 24-hour target coverage at 60 mg once daily. Phase 2 cardiovascular proof-of-concept data is now expected in H2 2026, positioning NLRP3 inhibition as the most advanced inflammaging therapeutic in clinical development.

title: GPX4 Senolytic Strategy Validated in Both Mice and First Human Trial
description: Imperial College's Nature Cell Biology paper on chloroacetamide GPX4 inhibitors converges with Rubedo's Phase 1 topical RLS-1496 data showing senescent-cell and epidermal thickness reductions. GPX4 is rapidly emerging as the most clinically tractable senolytic mechanism, with both academic and commercial pathways now active.

title: Creatine + Power Training Synergy Confirmed in Older Adults
description: A new Experimental Gerontology trial in 103 adults aged ~68 found creatine paired with high-load velocity-intentional resistance training amplified BDNF, antioxidant defense, and inflammation reductions over training alone. The result strengthens the practical case for adding 3-5g creatine daily to power-based strength protocols for healthy aging.

title: Q1 2026 Longevity Capital Tracks Toward $8-9B Breakout Year
description: Longevity.Technology's DLT dataset shows $3.74B raised across 49 deals in Q1 2026 — a 56% jump versus Q1 2025 — putting the full year on pace for $8-9B. Insilico-Lilly's $2.75B AI drug discovery deal and WHOOP's $575M raise are anchoring a maturing pipeline where median deal size has climbed to $21.8M.

title: BioAge Labs R&D Day: BGE-102 Phase 1 Shows Big hsCRP Drops
description: BioAge presented Phase 1 results for BGE-102 with 24-hour coverage at 60 mg once-daily dosing and profound hsCRP reductions in obese subjects, setting up cardiovascular and DME proof-of-concept studies.

title: Creatine Shows Synergy With Exercise in Older Adults
description: Spanish researchers in Experimental Gerontology showed that creatine combined with power training improved BDNF, antioxidant defense, inflammation, and lower-limb function in 103 older adults over 16 weeks.

title: May 2026 Clinical Studies in Aging Roundup
description: Active interventional studies surveyed include everolimus mTORC1 inhibition in metabolic-risk older adults (NCT05835999), RAP PAC rapalog pharmacology (NCT05949658), and a GLP-1 trial in postmenopausal women evaluating aging biomarkers.

title: Vitalist Bay 2026 Returns to Berkeley May 14-17
description: The Vitalism Foundation's pop-up longevity festival returns to Berkeley's Lighthaven campus May 14-17 with a four-day program spanning AI x Bio, Replacement, Investment, and Therapeutics tracks.

title: New Senolytic Strategy Targets GPX4 in Ageing Cells
description: Gil and Tate groups identify a new class of chloroacetamide senolytics that selectively kill senescent cells by inhibiting GPX4 and triggering ferroptosis, with promising data in melanoma, prostate, and ovarian cancer models.

title: Rubedo Reports Positive Phase 1 Results for RLS-1496
description: First-in-class topical GPX4 modulator was well tolerated with dose-related target engagement, reduction in senescent cells, lower inflammatory cytokines, and an average 20% reduction in epidermal thickness over 4 weeks.

title: Academic Clinical Trials for Rapamycin Aim to Answer Dosing Questions
description: Multi-phase academic rapamycin trial in older adults will run RCT cohort of ~84 participants comparing daily, intermittent, and placebo regimens to establish evidence-based dosing for healthy aging use.

title: Insilico Medicine and Eli Lilly Sign $2.75B AI Drug Discovery Pact
description: Lilly grants Insilico $115M upfront with up to $2.75B in milestones plus tiered royalties for exclusive worldwide license to a pipeline of oral preclinical assets, building on prior 2023 and 2025 collaborations.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup April 2026
description: Monthly digest covers replacement-over-repair strategy, inflammaging-epigenetic clock links, engineered stem-cell antibody factories, SRN-901 lifespan extension, and BCLXL-PROTAC senolytics for COPD.

title: Cellular Rejuvenation Has the Potential to Reverse Aging (NYT Magazine)
description: Long-form feature on partial reprogramming, Altos Labs, and the first safety trial of rejuvenation therapy in humans targeting glaucoma, situating cellular rejuvenation as the most promising frontier in longevity science.

title: Tozorakimab Meets Primary Endpoints in Two Phase 3 COPD Trials
description: AstraZeneca's IL-33 antibody tozorakimab achieved statistically significant reductions in moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations in OBERON and TITANIA, with MIRANDA and PROSPERO ongoing.

title: Junyue Cao on How the Body Ages, Cell by Cell
description: Rockefeller University researcher discusses ultra-high-throughput single-cell technologies applied to mapping the cellular hallmarks of aging across tissues.

title: How Intestinal Aging Encourages Harmful Bacteria
description: An Aging Cell study elucidates the bidirectional relationship between intestinal epithelial aging and age-related shifts in the gut microbiome that favor pathogenic species.

title: Thinking AI Outperforms Human Doctors on Real-Life Clinical Data
description: New benchmark study shows reasoning-capable LLMs outperform human physicians on real clinical decision tasks, with implications for AI-assisted longevity medicine and diagnostics.

title: Q1 2026 Longevity Biotech Investment Hits $3.74B Across 49 Deals
description: DLT chatbot projects 2026 full-year capital flows of $8-9B (55-60% YoY growth), with median deal size of $21.8M showing a maturing pipeline graduating from seed and early Series A.

title: The Longevity Gap: Americans Anticipate Longer Lives, Falling Behind on Readiness
description: Guardian's 2026 Mind, Body & Wallet report documents the lowest overall well-being and financial health levels in 15 years of tracking, despite rising lifespan expectations.

title: Insilico Forms Longevity Board to Oversee AI Aging Research
description: Insilico Medicine establishes a dedicated longevity scientific advisory board to direct AI-driven discovery within its aging-focused pipeline.

title: Inbrain Completes Enrollment in First Human Graphene Brain Interface Study
description: First-in-human study of graphene-based neural interfaces fully enrolled, supporting future applications in age-related neurodegeneration.

title: Seragon SRN-901 Extends Median Lifespan 33% in Aged Mice
description: SRN-901 multipathway longevity drug treatment of 18-month-old mice produced a 33% increase in median remaining lifespan plus reductions in frailty and tumor incidence compared to placebo.

title: Sleep Apnea, Intermittent Hypoxia, and p16-Mediated Cellular Senescence
description: Preprint links sleep apnea-induced intermittent hypoxia to p16Ink4a senescence accumulation in vascular endothelium, suggesting CPAP-resistant cardiovascular risk has a senescence component.

title: Spaceflight and Microgravity as a Model of Biological Aging
description: Review proposes spaceflight as a translational model for accelerated aging, leveraging muscle wasting, immune dysregulation, and bone loss signatures.

title: Small-Molecule IGF1R Inhibitors Extend Healthspan in Mice
description: Targeted small-molecule IGF1R inhibitors demonstrated healthspan extension in a mouse model, reinforcing IGF-1 signaling as a tractable geroprotector pathway.

title: BioAge Phase 2 BGE-102 Cardiovascular POC Data Expected H2 2026
description: BioAge confirms Phase 2 cardiovascular risk proof-of-concept data for BGE-102 anticipated H2 2026, with Phase 1b/2a diabetic macular edema readout in mid-2027.

title: Bryan Johnson Drops Rapamycin, Adds Lithium and NDGA to 2026 Stack
description: Project Blueprint's 2026 update drops rapamycin (stopped 2024), reduces NMN/NR to six days a week, and adds low-dose lithium and NDGA for cognitive and cellular support.

title: Peter Attia's April 2026 Research Worth Sharing — Endurance and Metabolic Inheritance
description: Attia spotlights new research on cardiorespiratory fitness, with cross-generational metabolic fitness implications for offspring beyond individual longevity benefits.
